title: Tejas, Akash, BrahMos & more: How India strengthened defence manufacturing over the last decade
content: India's defence production has surged to a record Rs 1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26, a significant leap towards self-reliance. Prime Minister Modi highlighted this achievement, showcasing indigenous platforms like Tejas, Akash, Agni-V, and INS Vikrant. This marks a major transformation from import dependence to a capable defence exporter.

title: Three years on, has Sri Lanka recovered from its economic collapse?
content: Sri Lanka, once a symbol of economic collapse, has achieved remarkable stabilisation. Fuel shortages and soaring inflation have receded, with economic growth returning and tourism booming. While international lenders praise the IMF-backed reforms, significant challenges like high public debt and low investment persist, indicating recovery is still incomplete and transformation is the next crucial step.

title: DOJ sides with Elon Musk's xAI in lawsuit seeking to stop natural gas turbines
content: The US Department of Justice has backed Elon Musk's xAI in a lawsuit concerning natural gas turbines, arguing their operation is crucial for national security and AI innovation supporting military operations. The DOJ's intervention supports xAI against a NAACP lawsuit alleging Clean Air Act violations due to unpermitted turbine use and potential health risks.

title: ISI-linked terror module busted: Delhi Police arrests five more operatives in TTH case
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell has apprehended five more individuals linked to a Pakistan-ISI-backed terror module, Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH). These arrests are part of a wider crackdown on a trans-border network allegedly involved in targeting police, spreading propaganda, and facilitating weapon and drug smuggling from Pakistan.

title: 'I killed her': Bengaluru man calls lawyer after strangling wife
content: Bengaluru police have arrested a water tanker driver, Sharath, accused of strangling his 20-year-old wife, Anusha, following frequent domestic quarrels. The accused allegedly confessed to the crime over a phone call to an advocate before fleeing their Malleswaram residence. Police discovered the victim's body after being alerted by the advocate and subsequently launched a manhunt for Sharath.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: Flight of the 'seagull': Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman in space 63 years ago today
content: On June 16, 1963, Valentina Tereshkova, a 26-year-old textile worker, became the first woman in space aboard Vostok 6. Her flight, a political statement by Nikita Khrushchev, lasted nearly three days, completing 48 orbits. Tereshkova's achievement, despite lacking formal pilot or engineering training, was celebrated globally, though she never flew again.

title: UK home prices rise 3.8% in April; North East England leads surge, London records decline
content: UK house prices saw a 3.8% year-on-year increase in April 2026, reaching an average of £270,000. Residential property transactions surged by 53.2%, indicating market recovery. While the North East led annual growth at 9.9%, London experienced a 2.1% annual decline despite a strong monthly rise.

title: Kishan eclipses Maxwell's famous Wankhede hundred, hits 71-ball ton to become ...
content: Kishan reached the milestone on the final ball of the 33rd over. He cut a delivery from Bilal Sami through backward point for a boundary to bring up his second ODI century.

title: Europe’s biggest pharma company Novo Nordisk ‘hacked’
content: Novo Nordisk, Europe's largest pharmaceutical company, has been targeted by cyber extortion group FulcrumSec. The group claims to have infiltrated Novo Nordisk's network for over two months, stealing more than a terabyte of sensitive data, including drug information and employee records.

title: Land acquired by govt? Here's what happens now & how compensation is calculated
content: India's land acquisition law, the 2013 Act, aims to ensure fair acquisition, compensation and transparent resettlement for affected families. It mandates Social Impact Assessments, public hearings, and consultation with local bodies before acquisition. The law details compensation, including market value multipliers and solatium, and outlines rehabilitation schemes for all dependent families, with special protections for tribal communities.

title: Meet Antonela Roccuzzo, Lionel Messi’s childhood love
content: Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo share one of sport's most enduring love stories, rooted in their shared childhood in Rosario, Argentina. They married in July 2017 and have three sons together: Thiago, Mateo, and Ciro. Roccuzzo has built a massive global following in her own right, known less for glamour and more for consistent family presence through every chapter of Messi's extraordinary career. As Messi competes at the 2026 World Cup, their family remains one of the most recognised in football.

title: 'Mom and dad, I love you': 23-year-old NEET aspirant dies by suicide in Dehradun, note recovered
content: A 23-year-old NEET aspirant, Ria, allegedly died by suicide at her Dehradun residence due to stress over the medical entrance exam re-examination. A brief suicide note was recovered, expressing love for her parents and frustration over academic struggles. Police are investigating, with family and neighbors reporting no significant behavioral changes.

title: New Delhi railway station: Guinness record, history and untold stories
content: New Delhi Railway Station, established in 1926, has evolved from a colonial transport hub into a global nexus. It significantly shaped Delhi's urban landscape, property markets, and economic growth by fostering neighbourhoods and driving migration. Its multimodal connectivity continues to boost real estate values, making it a vital economic anchor.

title: 'Fully prepared': Akhilesh dismisses Rajbhar's SP split claim, reminds BJP of its 'history'
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has dismissed claims of a major split within his party. He accused the Bharatiya Janata Party of a history of engineering defections in opposition parties. Yadav stated the Samajwadi Party is prepared for such challenges. These remarks come amid political positioning for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh assembly elections.

title: 'Discarding very old and bringing in new': Annamalai pitches party launch by July
content: K Annamalai, after leaving the BJP, is set to transform his 'We the Leaders' movement into a political party by July. With 18 lakh members already joined, the organization aims to bring fresh leadership and address state issues like the drug menace. The party plans to introduce term limits for politicians.

title: 'This is how we speak': Raut unapologetic over abusive remark against rebel MPs
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut defended his strong remarks against suspected rebel MPs, stating they are common Marathi usage and not abusive in the state's context. He questioned criticism, emphasizing that wrongdoing should be punished and vowed to fight any attempts to weaken the party.

title: Court orders summer camp to pay over Rs 50,000 after children find lizards in drinking water
content: A Hyderabad consumer court ordered summer camp organisers to refund fees with interest and pay compensation. Parents alleged unhygienic conditions, unsafe environments, and broken promises regarding accommodation, food, and medical care. The court found the organisers guilty of unfair trade practices for failing to provide promised facilities, leading to the significant ruling.

title: Hrithik Roshan's 'Alpha' cameo sparks fan frenzy: 'Kabir nation wake up'
content: YRF's 'Alpha' trailer has ignited fan frenzy with a fleeting glimpse of light-eyed character, widely believed to be Hrithik Roshan's 'War' spy, Major Kabir Dhaliwal. This brief appearance, despite Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's action, has dominated online discussions, fueling speculation about Kabir's pivotal role in the expanding Spy Universe and bridging upcoming films.

title: 'Targeting students, not leak networks': Rahul attacks Centre over Telegram ban
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized the Modi government's temporary ban on Telegram ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination, calling it a diversionary tactic targeting students instead of paper leak networks.

title: 'I would have dropped Sooryavanshi': Manjrekar slams teenager after on-field clash
content: Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar suggested dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from the India A squad as a disciplinary action following an on-field altercation with Sri Lanka A players. Despite provocations, Manjrekar emphasized that physical contact is unacceptable. The 15-year-old's recent form has also been inconsistent, contrasting with his stellar IPL 2026 performance.

title: Uddhav's Sena hit by fresh rebellion: 6 UBT MPs set to join Shinde camp; NDA to get boost in LS
content: Six Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MPs reportedly formed a separate group and submitted a letter to the Speaker, with plans to join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena on June 19. Despite Shiv Sena (UBT) leaders urging the Speaker not to recognize the split, sources claim the new group has been acknowledged.

title: Dana White reveals multiple threats behind historic UFC Freedom 250 at the White House
content: UFC President Dana White revealed that authorities monitored multiple security threats, including an alleged drone attack plot, before UFC Freedom 250 at the White House. Despite these concerns, the event proceeded safely, with White praising federal agencies for their work. He indicated the historic White House card is unlikely to be repeated due to immense logistical and financial challenges.

title: 'Rape gangs': UK MP demands deportation of Pakistani men linked to grooming gangs
content: A UK MP has revealed harrowing testimonies from grooming gang abuse survivors, alleging race and religion were used to justify their mistreatment. He plans to use parliamentary privilege to name perpetrators and enablers, while pursuing private prosecutions due to low faith in the justice system.

title: Brazilian company Embraer offers to build C-390 transport aircraft in India for IAF
content: In a strategic bid to enhance India's defense capabilities, Brazilian aircraft manufacturer Embraer has unveiled plans for a comprehensive assembly line for the C-390 Millennium in India, targeting the Air Force's Multi-Role Transport Aircraft (MTA) program.

title: Why is Nitish Kumar Reddy not playing the second ODI against Afghanistan?
content: Team India faces an injury blow as Nitish Kumar Reddy is ruled out of the second ODI against Afghanistan with a sore left thigh, forcing a reshuffle in the bowling attack. Afghanistan also deal with fitness concerns, making multiple changes as both teams aim for consistency and valuable lessons from the opening match.

title: 'Nitish Kumar has got everything done': Lalu Yadav on withdrawal of Z+ security cover
content: RJD supremo Lalu Prasad Yadav accused Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar of orchestrating the withdrawal of his and Rabri Devi's Z-plus security cover. Following the downgrade, RJD workers took over guarding Rabri Devi's residence, asserting their duty to protect her in the absence of Lalu and Tejashwi Yadav.

title: Brett Lee meets R Madhavan for a round of golf, praises his ‘decent swing’
content: Brett Lee met R Madhavan for a golf session and praised his skills and personality on social media. Their photos quickly went viral, delighting fans. Madhavan, currently earning acclaim for ‘Dhurandhar’, showcased his relaxed side. The film, led by Ranveer Singh, features a strong ensemble and is directed by Aditya Dhar.

title: Jio’s $4 billion IPO may be just days away: Here's what to expect
content: Reliance Jio is reportedly nearing the submission of draft papers for its substantial $4 billion IPO, a move anticipated before Mukesh Ambani's upcoming shareholder address. This potential listing, aiming for a fresh issue to fund Jio's growth, could become one of India's largest stock market debuts.

title: ‘Alpha’ trailer released: Alia Bhatt and Sharvari team up against the big bad
content: The trailer for 'Alpha,' starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, unveils the origin story of a young assassin. The spy thriller, set to release on July 3, 2026, features high-octane action and a compelling narrative of a princess rescuing herself, with Bobby Deol and Anil Kapoor in key roles. This marks the YRF spyverse's first female-led spy drama.

title: Namo Cities: How will NCR’s future-ready urban hubs look like
content: New 'Namo Cities' are being planned across the NCR, centred around high-speed Namo Bharat transit stations. These transit-oriented developments will integrate homes, offices, and leisure spaces, aiming to manage the region's rapid growth. A Rs 5,000 crore incentive package supports this vision, with states competing for locations.

title: After Sena turmoil, Samajwadi Party next? OP Rajbhar predicts rebellion in Akhilesh camp
content: Uttar Pradesh minister Om Prakash Rajbhar predicts a significant political realignment within the Samajwadi Party, alleging an impending internal split. He claims SP leaders are seeking to align with the BJP due to pressure from corruption cases, citing a purported letter from Ram Gopal Yadav to Amit Shah as evidence.

title: Telegram moves Delhi HC against Centre's ban ahead of NEET-UG re-test
content: Russian messaging platform Telegram has challenged the Centre's decision to ban the app in the Delhi High Court. The temporary ban was imposed ahead of the NEET-UG re-test due to concerns over exam-related content circulation. Telegram is seeking judicial intervention against the government's restriction.

title: 'Rs 50cr offered per MP': Uddhav's Sena on edge as 'Op Tiger' revives memories of 2022 rebellion
content: Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde is reportedly in touch with a group of Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MPs for 'Operation Tiger,' potentially leading to another split. This move, if successful, could significantly weaken Uddhav Thackeray's faction and bolster the NDA's parliamentary strength. Shiv Sena (UBT) leaders have dismissed the reports, dubbing the alleged move 'Operation Wolf'.

title: India may ask refiners to maintain larger crude oil inventories; here's why
content: The disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has challenged a long-standing belief among Indian policymakers that the country's proximity to the crude-rich Persian Gulf reduced the need for large strategic petroleum reserves.

title: FIFA WC: Austria end 36-year wait with dramatic victory over debutants Jordan
content: Austria secured a long-awaited World Cup victory, defeating debutants Jordan 3-1.  Romano Schmid opened the scoring, but Jordan equalised before an own goal and a late Marko Arnautovic penalty sealed the win. This historic triumph sets up a showdown with defending champions Argentina.

title: 'Nothing to do with football': Messi reveals why he cried after Argentina's win
content: Lionel Messi's emotional hat-trick in Argentina's 3-0 World Cup win over Algeria marked a historic night, equaling Miroslav Klose's all-time scoring record.  Despite personal difficulties, Messi delivered a masterful performance, celebrating his 200th international cap with a record-equalling feat.

title: White Paper addresses adverse impact of ‘revenue collapse’ on people of Tamil Nadu
content: The White Paper observed “the inter-generational transfer—from today’s beneficiaries of government services to tomorrow’s taxpayers who will service the resulting debt—is the defining fiscal inequity of the period under review.”

title: Ready to quit MLA post if TVK govt. reduces Tamil Nadu’s annual borrowing: ex-Finance Minister Thangam Thennarasu
content: On the issue of interest payments made by the Tamil Nadu government vis-à-vis the Gujarat government, Mr. Thennarasu said Gujarat was not implementing many of the welfare schemes that Tamil Nadu was

title: Odisha CM orders inquiry into 1,678 errors in school textbooks
content: A three-member high-level committee, headed by the Development Commissioner, has been instructed to probe the matter and submit its report within seven days

title: Speaker did not conduct ‘meaningful inquiry’ before accepting resignations of four MLAs: AIADMK whip tells Madras High Court
content: He says the submission of the first three resignations at 2.30 p.m. and their acceptance at 3.55 p.m. demonstrate a sham of compliance with constitutional procedures

title: Crime Branch takes over probe into death of nursing student in Kollam
content: Febina Sajan, a 23-year-old nursing student of a private nursing college in Kollam, was found dead on January 18, 2026

title: White Paper says Tamil Nadu’s power distribution company faces a “persistent monthly structural” cash shortfall
content: A monthly shortfall of ₹2,500 crore translates to an annual liquidity gap of about ₹30,000 crore, financed through a combination of short-term borrowings, delayed payments to power purchase counterparties, and deferred capital expenditure, says report

title: NIA attaches land of terror case accused in J&K's Baramulla
content: The investigation focusses on the illegal arms network, terror funding and the interception of Hizbul terrorists alongside former DSP Devender Singh

title: CM inaugurates Telangana Public School in Rangareddy
content: Telangana Govt committed to create infra for quality education and skilling to make youth job ready, says CM

title: Rahul Gandhi moves Supreme Court in defamation case
content: Mr. Gandhi also sought a stay of a Gujarat High Court judgment which upheld his conviction. He said the High Court verdict “has no parallel or precedent in the jurisprudence of the law of defamation”.

title: HD KSOE executives meet T.N. CM Vijay on proposed ₹38,000-crore shipbuilding project in Thoothukudi
content: During the meeting, the Chief Minister assured the executives that the Tamil Nadu government would extend all necessary support to ensure the speedy execution of the project

title: Body of custodial torture victim Akash Delison taken for cremation following court order
content: He died on March 8 reportedly due to custodial torture by the Manamadurai police; his family refused to receive his body

title: Ganja smuggling racket with international links busted in Anakapalli; 213 kg seized, five held
content: The Superintendent of Police said the key accused had a 2022 drug conviction in Sri Lanka, was jailed there, and entered India illegally by sea after his release on bail

title: Navi Mumbai Airport to launch international operations on July 15
content: Navi Mumbai International Airport Limited CEO B.V.J.K. Sharma said that both ‘Air India Express and IndiGo are going to operate international flights from Navi Mumbai’

title: Samajwadi Party trashes U.P. Minister Rajbhar’s claims on split in party
content: Om Prakash Rajbhar claims that the situation was not limited to Maharashtra and West Bengal and that "the entire SP is ready to join the BJP"

title: Hyderabad-based CtrlS secures ₹7,000 cr from Canada Pension Plan Investment Board
content: CPP Investments will acquire a 8.2% stake in CtrlS for ₹4,000 crore as well as invest ₹3,000 crore in a new joint venture with the Hyderabad-based company to develop hyperscale data centre campuses across India

title: Veteran Kannada actor Doddanna files cyber complaint over fake death rumours
content: The complaint comes after a social media account named “Public Friend” shared a post falsely claiming that the actor had passed away while offering condolences.

title: G7 Summit: Modi suggests global Skilled Mobility Partnership, Economic Corridor for Global South
content: India and countries of the Global South has young talent while many societies were ageing, the Prime Minister said, as he called for a “Global Skills Partnership”

title: India’s rainfall deficit at 35% as monsoon progress stalls
content: Central India faces 63% deficit as monsoon’s first round loses momentum; govt. directs crop-wise contingency plans, puts around 200 districts under priority monitoring, orders weekly reviews

title: RSETI training centre opens in Nandyal to help rural youth turn entrepreneurs
content: Officials said the Union Bank-run centre would give rural youth free vocational training and bank-loan support for self-employment

title: Sanjeev Arora case: Court bins Punjab minister's bail plea to prevent attempts to tamper with proof
content: Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora was arrested by the ED in connection with a money laundering case related to an alleged ₹100 crore GST fraud involving certain entities linked to him

title: Kerala Budget: Fiscal health, welfare, growth in focus as Congress-led UDF govt to present Revised Budget on June 19
content: Budget faces challenge of finding middle ground between re-aligning Kerala’s fiscal space for tackling concerns raised in White Paper on State finances and mobilising additional revenue for development and welfare, notably Indira Guarantees and ‘dream projects’

title: Karnataka police chief directs staff to strictly adhere to office timings
content: Referring to directions issued by Karnataka Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar, the circular stated that all government offices are required to commence functioning at 10 a.m.

title: SC says probe needed into ‘serious’ allegation of Registry misplacing case records
content: CJI Surya Kant says claim that records in an urgent matter went missing is a ‘very serious matter’ that warrants scrutiny

title: Bengaluru Development portfolio — the most coveted and contested
content: D.K. Shivakumar, who earlier held charge of Bengaluru Development as the Deputy Chief Minister, senior Bengaluru Congress leader Ramalinga Reddy, and Krishna Byre Gowda, have been wrangling for the portfolio and none seem to be happy with the situation.

title: Attack on Youth Congress activists: SIT approaches Kerala HC against anticipatory bail granted to accused police personnel
content: SIT contends that the restrictions on custodial interrogation have a serious impact on the investigation into the matter and requests the court to quash the anticipatory bail order

title: Hyderabad’s Dhruva Space, Finland’s ICEYE ink pact to collaborate on various fronts
content: Opportunities for large-scale space components manufacturing in India to be evaluated; earth observation services, disaster management solutions and ground segment infrastructure figure in the MoU announced at Bharat Innovates 2026 in France

title: Posters for Rahul Gandhi's Kota event removed, locals pressured not to attend: Pilot
content: Congress leader Sachin Pilot mentioned that Rahul Gandhi would engage with students in Kota to understand their issues and discuss ways to tackle the challenges faced by aspirants.

title: Nicobarese tribal councils oppose draft poll rules, say traditional system aligns with democratic values
content: The Andaman and Nicobar administration has called a meeting in Sri Vijaya Puram to discuss the draft rules on June 30; seeking withdrawal of these rules, the councils say their “traditional, time-tested” system is “consensus-based” and in practice for thousands of years

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Telegram app restricted in India at NTA request
content: The agency said that several Telegram groups were advertising fake exam papers for the upcoming NEET exam and defrauding candidates willing to pay for them

title: Tesla plans Supercharger network in Hyderabad, bets on home charging to drive India’s EV future
content: Tesla opens its fifth Experience Center in India at Knowledge City, Hyderabad. The 2026 Model Y Premium Rear-Wheel Drive is priced at ₹50.89 lakh and the six-seater Model Y L is priced from ₹61.99 lakh

title: Four ex-AIADMK MLAs facing disqualification proceedings submit replies to Tamil Nadu Speaker’s notices
content: Disqualification proceedings were initiated against former MLAs S. Jayakumar (Perundurai), Maragatham Kumaravel (Madurantakam), P. Sathyabama (Dharapuram) and Esakki Subaya (Ambasamudram) for defying the party whip

title: Cloudy skies, gusty winds over Visakhapatnam; IMD forecasts thunderstorms
content: The weather office warned of lightning and winds of 40 to 50 kmph at isolated places, the day’s high touching 39.9 degrees Celsius in parts of the State

title: Practise yoga daily for a healthy life, Nandyal Collector and SP tell residents
content: The event drew students, government employees and the public, who performed asanas, pranayama and meditation

title: Strong heroines, mellow heroes, engaging stories and fierce social commentary define Bharathiraja’s repertoire
content: With fresh eyes, groomed on a rural story line in the lush surroundings of Theni in south Tamil Nadu, Bharathiraja decided he was done with the conventional tropes of the hero, the baddie, and a hanger-on heroine

title: Eknath Shinde, Shrikant Shinde in Delhi amid ‘Operation Tiger’ buzz
content: Sources close to Eknath Shinde said that the breakaway MPs from Shiv Sena (UBT) had submitted a letter to the Speaker’s office on June 16

title: The best mangoes in South India, according to farmers who grow them
content: We ask farmers across Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Telangana one question: Which mango would you choose above all others? From Tamil Nadu’s Sappattai to Karnataka’s Appemidi, they reveal the fruits they cherish most — and why preserving heirloom mangoes matters

title: BJP won’t succeed in its ultimate objective, says Congress amid Shiv Sena (UBT) developments
content: The Home Minister is running a wholly cynical campaign that is well lubricated and, like in the mutual funds industry, offers various schemes and products suited to individual needs. There are absolutely no limits to his depravity, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: IIT-Delhi study finds human activity drives India’s ‘wild’ weather
content: ‘Historical rainfall statistics alone may not provide a reliable guide to future risk in a warming climate,’ a researcher said

title: Bengaluru woman murdered by husband, even as friend slept next to her
content: The police registered a case of murder and arrested Kiran, 31, within hours. Kiran said that Navyashri and he used to have frequent fights over alleged extramarital relationships.

title: Man ends life after killing live-in partner in Bengaluru
content: A 51-year-old private firm employee allegedly strangled his live-in partner to death and later ended his life at their residence near Indira Priyadarshini Nagar, under the Rajagopalnagar police station limits

title: TVK government’s White Paper puts Tamil Nadu’s debt at ₹13.18 lakh crore
content: The report says the headline debt figure of ₹10 lakh crore in 2025-26 captures only the State’s direct borrowings through market loans, institutional debt, and public account liabilities; it does not capture the borrowings of the PSUs, statutory boards, and special purpose vehicles that the State guarantees or implicitly supports

title: 12 final-year BDS students of Hyderabad college suspended for six months over ragging allegations
content: The first-year students complained that the seniors routinely ordered them to bring alcohol, cigarettes, food items and pick up Swiggy deliveries, besides making them perform various tasks, said Principal of Government Dental College in Hyderabad

title: Kerala govt’s White Paper on fiscal health seeks revamp of loss-making public sector enterprises
content: Document calls for reform of KSEB, KSRTC and KWA, recommends merger of Kerala State Beverages Corporation and Kerala State Civil Supplies Corporation (Supplyco)

title: Tamil Nadu’s own-tax effort has ‘collapsed’, says White Paper
content: Giving an account of how T.N.’s own tax revenue fared vis-à-vis 3 ‘peer States’ in last 5 years, the report says the SOTR-to-GSDP ratio declined from 5.93% to 5.45%, the ‘lowest in the State’s history and the steepest decline’ among the benchmarked States

title: Former minister K.S. Eshwarappa criticises Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge for RSS queries
content: He challenged Priyank Kharge to act against RSS as Home Minister as per law, rather than raise questions

title: T. Veena, daughter of ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, appears before ED for questioning in CMRL pay-off case
content: This is the first time Veena is appearing before ED at its office in the probe linked to allegations that CMRL made payments amounting to ₹2.78 crore to her now-defunct IT firm Exalogic without receiving any services in return

title: Assam's Don Bosco University Sacks Teacher After Sexual Misconduct Allegation
content: Reports said a second-semester woman student had accused Chandra - the head of Department of Public Administration -- of inappropriate behaviour and sexual misconduct.

title: Why Meta Is Suddenly Charging For Facebook, Instagram And WhatsApp
content: Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp have long been free. So why is Meta suddenly asking users to pay? The answer may lie in Mark Zuckerberg's expensive AI ambitions.

title: Cyber Criminals Like Parasites: Chief Justice Surya Kant While Denying Bail
content: "You (cyber criminals) are parasites. You take money from investors and dupe them. We have to be very harsh on cyber criminals," Justice Kant said.

title: A WhatsApp Text From 'Boss' Costs Company Over Rs 10 Crore
content: On June 3, Girish Amin, Deputy General Manager at INOX received a text from an unknown number claiming to be Siddharth Jain, the Executive Director.

title: Baby Born On Train In Assam, Nurse Traveler, Railway Team Help With Delivery
content: After the train reached Rangiya, the mother and newborn were attended to by the waiting medical team and shifted to Rangiya Railway Hospital for immediate care.

title: $14-Million Clean-Up Fails As Algae Returns To White House Reflecting Pool
content: The objective was to repair the pool, improve its structure, and make the water look clean and visually striking, with a blue tone described as "American Flag Blue," but the pool turned bright green again.

title: On Camera, Pet Rottweiler Attacks Boy In Bengaluru, Owner Faces Police Case
content: A 12-year-old boy was attacked and bitten by a pet Rottweiler in Bengaluru's Mahadevapura area, following which police filed a case against the dog's owner for alleged negligence.

title: Israeli Envoy Claims Hamas Network Is Expanding Into Pakistan And Bangladesh
content: Speaking on Pakistan's recent attempts to position itself as a bridge between the United States and Iran, Israeli Ambassador to India, Reuven Azar, said Israel does not trust Pakistan and views its diplomatic posture with caution.

title: Indian Man Opens Savings Account In UAE, Gets Rs 5-Crore Lucky Draw Win
content: The SIB launched the "Millionaire" campaign -- a savings-linked rewards programme-- in 2025 to encourage customers to build wealth through eligible deposit accounts.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Closes 347 Points Higher As Oil Falls To A Three-Month-Low
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent Oil fell another 4.4% to $79.53. Oil prices are down 16% from the high made 5 days ago.

title: VD Satheesan vs Pinarayi Vijayan Over 3 Vice Chancellors At RSS Event
content: VD Satheesan said that unlike the previous government, he had immediately termed the latest incident "unfortunate" and publicly condemned it. His remarks came after Vijayan said that the government's response was inadequate and that the matter was a

title: Deal Done, But Iran Can Still Restrict Access To Hormuz, Flags US Intel
content: Sources familiar with the findings of US intelligence assessments noted that Iran now has the capability to effectively restrict or shut access to the Strait of Hormuz at will.

title: Weather Today Live Updates: Delhi Records Coolest Day Of June As Rain Brings Respite From Heat
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain Alert Live Updates: Delhi NCR experiences cooler weather due to rain and thunderstorm

title: Why Iran Is Calling Its New US Agreement A Victory Despite Heavy Losses
content: Tehran said it has come out stronger after the recent conflict, and even claimed that it has shown more confidence than before the war began on February 28.

title: Jeffrey Epstein's Final 35 Days: Inside The Untold Story Behind His Death
content: Epstein had already been prosecuted once in Florida in 2008. In that earlier case, he received a controversial plea deal and served about 13 months in custody.

title: Video: Plane Crashes On Texas Highway, Cockpit Window Broken For Rescue
content: Police say six people were onboard and one was killed. The plane crashed in Laredo shortly after 10 pm.

title: Lawrence Bishnoi Gang Member Who Shot At AP Dhillon's Canada Home To Be Deported To India
content: The Canadian Immigration and Refugee Board cleared the way for Abjeet Kingra's deportation - who is currently serving a six-year sentence and is also facing charges related to another attack.

title: CMRL pay-off case involving ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s daughter T. Veena | Explained
content: Income Tax Interim Settlement Board in 2023 found that T. Veena and her now-defunct company Exalogic Solutions had received monthly payments from CMRL despite there being no evidence of any services given in return. A probe by Serious Fraud Investigation Office revealed fictitious cash expenses of about ₹182 crore over a span of 15 years, allegedly used by CMRL to bribe various persons

title: How everyday Kerala is being reimagined in an award-nominated illustration series
content: llustrator Mohammed Sajid’s portrait series is in contention at the AOI World Illustration Awards 2026, spotlighting fish sellers, flower vendors, postmen and other familiar faces of Kerala’s streets

title: US drops 'Indo' from Indo-Pacific Command name, cites 'restoring legacy'
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock.
